evals.expected_output: A scout-report.md file at the repo root with all 6 sections: What Is This, System Architecture, Design Philosophy, Component Catalog, Key Files, Non-Obvious Things. Then a prompt asking Daniel to read and say ready.
assertions: report contains a System Architecture section
report contains a Component Catalog section (table format)
report contains a Design Philosophy section derived from actual files (CLAUDE.md, core-beliefs.md, etc)
report contains a Key Files section with specific file names
report contains a Non-Obvious Things section
model waits for user to say 'ready' before continuing

evals.prompt: help me understand the guya-plugin/skills directory — what skills exist and how are they structured
evals.expected_output: Scoped report on just the skills directory: lists all skills with descriptions, explains SKILL.md structure, explains the anatomy pattern (scripts/, references/, assets/).
assertions: scout-report.md or equivalent output is written to disk
all skills in guya-plugin/skills/ are listed
skill anatomy (scripts/, references/, assets/) is described

evals.prompt: onboard me to this codebase, I've never seen it before
evals.expected_output: Same as eval 1 — full report + interactive session prompt. Tests that natural-language phrasing triggers the same structured flow.
assertions: report has at least 5 of the 6 required sections
interactive session is offered after report
report cites actual file names from the codebase (not generic placeholders)
